title: Alchi Monastery
tagline: Alchi Monastery — spiritual town of Ladakh (UT)

overview.short: Alchi Monastery (Tibetan: ཨ་ལྕི་ཆོས་འཁོར།) or Alchi Gompa (Tibetan: ཨ་ལྕི་དགོམ་པ།, also Alci) is a Tibetan Buddhist monastery, known more as a monastic complex (chos-'khor) of temp
overview.description: Alchi Monastery (Tibetan: ཨ་ལྕི་ཆོས་འཁོར།) or Alchi Gompa (Tibetan: ཨ་ལྕི་དགོམ་པ།, also Alci) is a Tibetan Buddhist monastery, known more as a monastic complex (chos-'khor) of temples in Alchi village in the Leh District, under the Ladakh Autonomous Hill Development Council of the Union Territory of Ladakh, India. The complex comprises four separate settlements in the Alchi village in the lower Ladakh region with monuments dated to different periods. Of these four hamlets, Alchi monastery is said to be the oldest and most famous. It is administered by the Likir Monastery.

topPlaces.name: Nimoo Bazgo Hydroelectric Plant
topPlaces.description: The Nimoo Bazgo Power Project is a run-of-the-river power project on the Indus River situated at Alchi village, 75 kilometres (47 mi) from Leh in the Indian Union Territory of Ladakh. The project was conceived on 1 July 2001 and approved on 8 June 2005, and construction began on 23rd Sept, 2006.

topPlaces.name: Alchi
topPlaces.description: Alchi is a village in the Sham district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Likir tehsil, on the banks of the Indus River 70 km (43.49miles) downstream from the capital Leh.

topPlaces.name: Saspol Caves
topPlaces.description: Saspol Caves (a.k.a. Gon-Nila-Phuk Cave Temples) are situated in the hills behind Suspol, Ladakh, India, which is about 76 km from the city centre of Leh.

topPlaces.name: Likir Monastery
topPlaces.description: Likir Monastery or Likir Gompa (Klud-kyil) is a Buddhist monastery in Ladakh, Northern India. It is located at 3700m elevation, approximately 52 kilometres (32 mi) west of Leh.

topPlaces.name: Rizong Monastery
topPlaces.description: Rizong (or Rhizong) gompa, Gelugpa or Yellow Hat Buddhist monastery, also called the Yuma Changchubling, is in Ladakh, India. It is situated at the top of a rocky side valley on the north side of the Indus, to the west of Alchi on the way to Lamayuru.

topPlaces.name: Likir
topPlaces.description: Likir is a village and eponymous Subdivision in the Sham district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Likir tehsil, in the Ladakh region.

topPlaces.name: Giramangu
topPlaces.description: Giramangu, also known as Gaira Mangue or Mangyu, is a village in the Sham district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Likir tehsil.

topPlaces.name: Basgo
topPlaces.description: Basgo or Bazgoo, is a village situated on the bank of the Indus River in Leh district, Ladakh, India. It was once an important cultural and political centre and is frequently mentioned in the Ladakhi Chronicles.

The project was conceived on 1 July 2001 and approved on 8 June 2005, and construction began on 23rd Sept, 2006.

Saspol Caves (a.k.a. Gon-Nila-Phuk Cave Temples) are situated in the hills behind Suspol, Ladakh, India, which is about 76 km from the city centre of Leh.
Likir Monastery or Likir Gompa (Klud-kyil) is a Buddhist monastery in Ladakh, Northern India. It is located at 3700m elevation, approximately 52 kilometres (32 mi) west of Leh.

Rizong (or Rhizong) gompa, Gelugpa or Yellow Hat Buddhist monastery, also called the Yuma Changchubling, is in Ladakh, India. It is situated at the top of a rocky side valley on the north side of the Indus, to the west of Alchi on the way to Lamayuru.
Likir is a village and eponymous Subdivision in the Sham district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Likir tehsil, in the Ladakh region.
Giramangu, also known as Gaira Mangue or Mangyu, is a village in the Sham district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Likir tehsil.

Basgo or Bazgoo, is a village situated on the bank of the Indus River in Leh district, Ladakh, India. It was once an important cultural and political centre and is frequently mentioned in the Ladakhi Chronicles.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Alchi Monastery?
faq.a: The best time to visit Alchi Monastery is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Alchi Monastery?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kushok Bakula Rimpochee Airport, Leh (36 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (248 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Alchi Monastery?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Alchi Monastery at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Alchi Monastery?
faq.a: Stays in Alchi Monastery start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Alchi Monastery?
faq.a: The top attractions are Nimoo Bazgo Hydroelectric Plant, Alchi, Saspol Caves, Likir Monastery, Rizong Monastery.
